pred_sqls:
WITH updated_transactions AS (
    UPDATE transactions_1k 
    SET Amount = 100 
    FROM (
        SELECT TransactionID 
        FROM transactions_1k 
        WHERE Amount = 50 
        ORDER BY Date 
        LIMIT 100 
        FOR UPDATE
    ) sub 
    WHERE transactions_1k.TransactionID = sub.TransactionID 
    RETURNING transactions_1k.*
)
SELECT ut.transactionid, ut.amount, ut.date, c.segment, c.currency
FROM updated_transactions ut
JOIN customers c ON ut.customerid = c.customerid;

SELECT 
    account_id, 
    MAX(amount) AS max_transaction, 
    MIN(amount) AS min_transaction,
    MAX(amount) - MIN(amount) AS amount_difference
FROM 
    trans
GROUP BY 
    account_id 
HAVING 
    COUNT(*) >= 2 
    AND (MAX(amount) - MIN(amount)) > 12000
ORDER BY 
    amount_difference DESC;

-- Option 1: Create a new unique index that matches your ON CONFLICT clause
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX unique_event ON event(event_name, location, event_date) 
WHERE event_name IS NOT NULL AND location IS NOT NULL;

-- Then your UPSERT would work:
INSERT INTO event (event_id, event_name, location, event_date) 
VALUES ('test1', 'test_name', 'test_location', 'test_date')
ON CONFLICT (event_name, location, event_date) 
DO UPDATE SET 
  event_id = 'test1', 
  event_name = 'test_name', 
  location = 'test_location', 
  event_date = 'test_date';

-- OR Option 2: Modify your UPSERT to use one of your existing constraints
INSERT INTO event (event_id, event_name, location, event_date) 
VALUES ('test1', 'test_name', 'test_location', 'test_date')
ON CONFLICT (event_name, event_date) 
DO UPDATE SET 
  event_id = 'test1', 
  event_name = 'test_name', 
  location = 'test_location', 
  event_date = 'test_date';
SELECT to_char(creationdate, 'FMH:MI:SS') FROM comments;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_loan_summary()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update the loan summary table with the correct count of active loans
    INSERT INTO loan_summary (account_id, total_loan_count, last_status_change)
    VALUES (NEW.account_id, 
           (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan WHERE account_id = NEW.account_id AND status = 'A'),
           CURRENT_DATE)
    ON CONFLICT (account_id) 
    DO UPDATE SET 
        total_loan_count = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM loan WHERE account_id = NEW.account_id AND status = 'A'),
        last_status_change = CURRENT_DATE,
        last_updated = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP;
    
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE TRIGGER tr_update_loan_summary
AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE OR DELETE ON loan
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION update_loan_summary();

SELECT u.id, 
       u.displayname,
       COUNT(DISTINCT pl.relatedpostid) as premium_referrals
FROM users u
LEFT JOIN posts p ON u.id = p.owneruserid
LEFT JOIN postlinks pl ON p.id = pl.postid
LEFT JOIN posts referred_post ON pl.relatedpostid = referred_post.id
LEFT JOIN users referred_user ON referred_post.owneruserid = referred_user.id
LEFT JOIN badges b ON referred_user.id = b.userid AND b.name = 'Premium'
WHERE b.id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY u.id, u.displayname
ORDER BY premium_referrals DESC;

INSERT INTO attendance (link_to_event, link_to_member, attend)
VALUES ('recEVTik3MlqbvLFi', 'rec280Sk7o31iG0Tx', 1)
ON CONFLICT (link_to_event, link_to_member) 
DO UPDATE SET attend = EXCLUDED.attend;

WITH attendance_counts AS (
    SELECT link_to_event, COUNT(link_to_member) AS attendance_count 
    FROM attendance 
    GROUP BY link_to_event
)
SELECT 
    e.event_id,
    e.event_name,
    ac.attendance_count AS attendance_table_count,
    b.amount AS budget_attendee_count
FROM event e
JOIN attendance_counts ac ON e.event_id = ac.link_to_event
JOIN budget b ON e.event_id = b.link_to_event
WHERE ac.attendance_count != b.amount
AND b.category = 'Attendees';

SELECT artist, 
       COUNT(*) as card_count,
       STRING_AGG(name, ', ' ORDER BY name) as card_names,
       COUNT(DISTINCT bordercolor) as unique_border_colors
FROM cards
GROUP BY artist
ORDER BY artist;

-- First, add a new timestamp column if you want to keep the original data
ALTER TABLE account ADD COLUMN date_timestamp TIMESTAMP;

-- Then update the new column with converted values
UPDATE account
SET date_timestamp = TO_TIMESTAMP(
    CASE 
        WHEN date < 500101 THEN '20' || TO_CHAR(date, 'FM000000') 
        ELSE '19' || TO_CHAR(date, 'FM000000')
    END, 
    'YYYYMMDD'
);

SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT * FROM transactions_1k 
    WHERE CustomerID = 3 AND Date = '2012-08-24'
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT * FROM transactions_1k 
    WHERE CustomerID = 7626 AND Date = '2012-08-24'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM transactions_1k 
        WHERE CustomerID = 3 AND Date = '2012-08-24'
    )
) results

WITH user_refs AS (
    -- Combine all references for each user
    SELECT id, username, ref1, ref2
    FROM users
),
asset_owners AS (
    -- Get all assets with their owners
    SELECT a.id, a.name, a.owner
    FROM assets a
),
user_groups AS (
    -- Create connected groups of users based on shared references
    SELECT 
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.ref1) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ur.ref2) AS refs2,
        COALESCE(ao.owner, ur.ref1, ur.ref2) AS owner_ref
    FROM user_refs ur
    LEFT JOIN asset_owners ao ON ao.owner = ur.ref1 OR ao.owner = ur.ref2
    GROUP BY COALESCE(ao.owner, ur.ref1, ur.ref2)
)
SELECT 
    ug.ids,
    ug.usernames,
    ug.refs1,
    ug.refs2,
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count,
    ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_ids
FROM user_groups ug
LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = ug.owner_ref
GROUP BY ug.ids, ug.usernames, ug.refs1, ug.refs2, ug.owner_ref
ORDER BY MIN(COALESCE(a.id, 0));

WITH priority_accounts AS (
  SELECT
    account.account_id,
    account.frequency,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    0 AS priority
  FROM account
  LEFT JOIN loan l
    ON account.account_id = l.account_id
  WHERE account.district_id = '18'
    AND l.date >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
SELECT * FROM priority_accounts
UNION ALL
SELECT
  account.account_id,
  account.frequency,
  l.loan_id,
  l.date AS loan_date,
  1 AS priority
FROM account
LEFT JOIN loan l
  ON account.account_id = l.account_id
WHERE account.district_id = '18'
  AND account.account_id NOT IN (SELECT account_id FROM priority_accounts)
ORDER BY priority, loan_date DESC NULLS LAST;

SELECT 'SELECT ' || array_to_string(ARRAY(
    SELECT 'o.' || c.column_name
    FROM information_schema.columns AS c
    WHERE c.table_name = 'account' 
    AND c.column_name NOT IN('account_id', 'date')
), ', ') || ' FROM account AS o' AS sqlstmt

SELECT l.*,
       MAX(amount) OVER(PARTITION BY account_id) AS max_loan_amount
FROM loan l
ORDER BY account_id, loan_id;

WITH date_range AS (
  SELECT 
    date_trunc('year', MIN(date))::date AS min_year,
    date_trunc('year', MAX(date))::date AS max_year
  FROM loan
)
SELECT 
  to_char(generate_series, 'YYYY') AS year
FROM 
  date_range,
  generate_series(
    min_year, 
    max_year, 
    interval '1 year'
  );

CREATE TABLE client_information (
    client_id smallserial NOT NULL,
    first_name character varying(50),
    last_name character varying(50),
    full_name character varying(100) GENERATED ALWAYS AS (first_name || ' ' || last_name) STORED,
    PRIMARY KEY (client_id)
);

SELECT 
  link_to_event,
  array_agg(link_to_member) AS members
FROM attendance
GROUP BY link_to_event;

WITH operation AS (
    INSERT INTO account (account_id, district_id, frequency, date) 
    VALUES (1, 18, 'POPLATEK MESICNE', CURRENT_DATE) 
    ON CONFLICT (account_id) DO UPDATE 
    SET date = CURRENT_DATE
    RETURNING account_id, 'INSERT' as operation_type
    WHERE xmax = 0
    UNION ALL
    SELECT account_id, 'UPDATE' as operation_type
    FROM account
    WHERE account_id = 1
    AND xmax <> 0
    AND xmin = (SELECT txid_current())
)
SELECT * FROM operation;

WITH operation AS (
    INSERT INTO account (account_id, district_id, frequency, date) 
    VALUES (1, 18, 'POPLATEK MESICNE', CURRENT_DATE) 
    ON CONFLICT (account_id) DO UPDATE 
    SET date = CURRENT_DATE, 
        district_id = EXCLUDED.district_id,
        frequency = EXCLUDED.frequency
    RETURNING account_id, 
              CASE WHEN xmax = 0 THEN 'INSERT' ELSE 'UPDATE' END as operation_type
)
SELECT * FROM operation;

WITH distinct_dates AS (
    -- Get distinct release dates
    SELECT DISTINCT releasedate 
    FROM sets_releaseinfo
),
date_groups AS (
    -- Identify groups of consecutive dates
    SELECT 
        releasedate,
        releasedate - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY releasedate))::integer AS date_group
    FROM distinct_dates
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS consecutive_days,
    MIN(releasedate) AS start_date,
    MAX(releasedate) AS end_date
FROM date_groups
GROUP BY date_group
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY consecutive_days DESC, start_date;

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- First, identify cards that have no previous card (starting points)
starting_points AS (
    SELECT id
    FROM collection
    WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT nextCardId FROM collection WHERE nextCardId IS NOT NULL)
),
-- Then build paths starting from these cards
path_cte AS (
    -- Base case: start with cards that have no previous card
    SELECT id, nextCardId, id::TEXT AS path
    FROM collection
    WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM starting_points)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: follow the nextCardId links
    SELECT p.id, c.nextCardId, p.path || ' --> ' || c.nextCardId::TEXT
    FROM path_cte p
    JOIN collection c ON p.nextCardId = c.id
    WHERE c.nextCardId IS NOT NULL
)
-- Select the complete paths (those that end with a card having no next card)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN c.nextCardId IS NULL THEN path
        ELSE path || ' --> ' || c.nextCardId::TEXT
    END AS complete_path
FROM path_cte p
JOIN collection c ON p.nextCardId = c.id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 
    FROM path_cte p2 
    WHERE p2.id = p.id AND LENGTH(p2.path) > LENGTH(p.path)
)
ORDER BY SPLIT_PART(path, ' --> ', 1)::INTEGER;

SELECT 
    t.trans_id,
    t.account_id,
    t.date,
    t.bank,
    (SELECT nt.bank 
     FROM trans nt 
     WHERE nt.account_id = t.account_id 
       AND nt.date >= t.date 
       AND nt.bank IS NOT NULL
     ORDER BY nt.date ASC
     LIMIT 1) AS next_bank
FROM trans t
ORDER BY t.account_id, t.date;

SELECT 
    t.*,
    (SELECT nt.bank 
     FROM trans nt 
     WHERE nt.account_id = t.account_id 
       AND nt.date >= t.date 
       AND nt.bank IS NOT NULL
     ORDER BY nt.date ASC
     LIMIT 1) AS next_bank
FROM trans t
ORDER BY t.account_id, t.date;

SELECT 
    tc.table_schema || '.' || tc.table_name AS referencing_table,
    kcu.column_name AS referencing_column,
    ccu.table_schema || '.' || ccu.table_name AS referenced_table,
    ccu.column_name AS referenced_column,
    tc.constraint_name,
    pg_get_constraintdef(pgc.oid) AS constraint_definition
FROM 
    information_schema.table_constraints tc
JOIN 
    information_schema.key_column_usage kcu
    ON tc.constraint_name = kcu.constraint_name
    AND tc.table_schema = kcu.table_schema
JOIN 
    information_schema.constraint_column_usage ccu
    ON ccu.constraint_name = tc.constraint_name
    AND ccu.table_schema = tc.table_schema
JOIN 
    pg_constraint pgc
    ON pgc.conname = tc.constraint_name
WHERE 
    tc.constraint_type = 'FOREIGN KEY' 
    AND ccu.table_name = 'users'
ORDER BY 
    tc.table_name, 
    kcu.column_name;

SELECT t.team_api_id, t.team_short_name, ta.buildupplayspeed
FROM team t
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT team_api_id, buildupplayspeed
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE (team_api_id, date) IN (
        SELECT team_api_id, MAX(date) as latest_date
        FROM team_attributes
        GROUP BY team_api_id
    )
) ta ON t.team_api_id = ta.team_api_id;

WITH combined_data AS (
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as action_year, 'transaction' as type
    FROM trans
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date_trunc('year', date) as action_year, 'loan' as type
    FROM loan
)
SELECT 
    action_year as year, 
    COUNT(*) as count
FROM combined_data
GROUP BY action_year
ORDER BY action_year;

WITH expanded_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        t.CustomerID, 
        t.Amount,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            ORDER BY t.CustomerID, t.Amount, t.TransactionID
        ) AS overall_row_num
    FROM transactions_1k t
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(1, CAST(t.Amount AS INTEGER)) AS gs(n)
)
SELECT 
    CustomerID, 
    Amount,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY overall_row_num) AS rn
FROM expanded_transactions
WHERE overall_row_num <= 5
ORDER BY overall_row_num;

SELECT 
    t.k_symbol, 
    t.operation, 
    t.amount, 
    t.balance, 
    a.frequency 
FROM trans t
INNER JOIN account a ON t.account_id = a.account_id
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT 
        k_symbol, 
        MIN(date) as min_date
    FROM trans
    WHERE account_id = 1 AND type = 'PRIJEM'
    GROUP BY k_symbol
) min_dates ON t.k_symbol = min_dates.k_symbol AND t.date = min_dates.min_date
WHERE t.account_id = 1 AND t.type = 'PRIJEM'

WITH purchase_months AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS month_date
    FROM purchase
    GROUP BY user_id, purchase_date
),
streaks AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        month_date,
        month_date - (INTERVAL '1 month' * ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date)) AS streak_group
    FROM purchase_months
),
streak_lengths AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streaks
    GROUP BY user_id, streak_group
),
max_streaks AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        MAX(streak_length) AS max_streak_length
    FROM streak_lengths
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT
    max_streak_length AS streak_length,
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM max_streaks
GROUP BY max_streak_length
ORDER BY max_streak_length DESC;

WITH LatestRatings AS (
    SELECT 
        player_api_id,
        date,
        overall_rating,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_api_id ORDER BY date DESC) as rn
    FROM Player_Attributes
)
SELECT 
    player_api_id,
    date,
    overall_rating
FROM LatestRatings
WHERE rn = 1;

SELECT pa.player_api_id, pa.date, pa.overall_rating
FROM Player_Attributes pa
INNER JOIN (
    SELECT player_api_id, MAX(date) as max_date
    FROM Player_Attributes
    GROUP BY player_api_id
) latest ON pa.player_api_id = latest.player_api_id AND pa.date = latest.max_date;

WITH tab_with_cardid AS (
   SELECT 
       cardid,
       -- Extract pure_cardid by removing the substring between first and second hyphens
       CONCAT(
           SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 1),
           '-',
           SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 3),
           CASE 
               WHEN SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 4) != '' THEN CONCAT('-', SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 4)) 
               ELSE '' 
           END,
           CASE 
               WHEN SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 5) != '' THEN CONCAT('-', SPLIT_PART(cardid, '-', 5)) 
               ELSE '' 
           END
       ) AS pure_cardid,
       type,
       last_refresh_date
    FROM card_info
), ranked_records AS (
    SELECT 
        cardid, 
        pure_cardid,
        type,
        last_refresh_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY pure_cardid ORDER BY type) AS rn
    FROM tab_with_cardid
)
SELECT 
    cardid, 
    pure_cardid,
    type,
    last_refresh_date
FROM ranked_records
WHERE rn = 1;

SELECT 
    id,
    displayname,
    CASE 
        WHEN age ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN age::numeric 
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS numeric_age
FROM users
WHERE age IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, displayname, age FROM users;

BEGIN
    INSERT INTO profiles (id, creationdate, websiteurl) 
    SELECT 
        NEW.id::varchar, 
        NEW.creationdate::text, 
        CASE 
            WHEN NEW.websiteurl IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE substring(NEW.websiteurl from 'http://([^.]+)')
        END;
    RETURN NEW;
END;

SELECT *,
    (CASE WHEN availability = 'paper' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN bordercolor = 'black' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN rarity = 'uncommon' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
     CASE WHEN type LIKE '%Creature%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matching_criteria_count
FROM cards
WHERE (CASE WHEN availability = 'paper' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN bordercolor = 'black' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN rarity = 'uncommon' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
       CASE WHEN type LIKE '%Creature%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) >= 3
ORDER BY matching_criteria_count DESC;

WITH basic_ny_users AS (
    SELECT user_id, email, SUM(revenue) as total_revenue
    FROM orders
    WHERE segment = 'basic' AND destination = 'New York'
    GROUP BY user_id, email
    HAVING SUM(revenue) > 2000
),
miami_users AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT user_id
    FROM orders
    WHERE destination = 'Miami'
)
SELECT DISTINCT user_id, email
FROM orders
WHERE 
    -- Condition 1: Luxury segment in New York
    (segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'New York')
    OR 
    -- Condition 2: Luxury segment in London
    (segment = 'luxury' AND destination = 'London')
UNION
-- Condition 3: Basic segment in New York with revenue > $2000
SELECT user_id, email
FROM basic_ny_users
UNION
-- Condition 4: Users who never purchased in Miami
SELECT DISTINCT user_id, email
FROM orders
WHERE user_id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM miami_users)

ALTER TABLE hero_power ADD CONSTRAINT unique_active_hero_power 
UNIQUE (hero_id, power_id, (CASE WHEN active = true THEN 1 ELSE random() END));

SELECT userid, tag
FROM preference_tag
WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM unnest(tag) AS t WHERE t LIKE '%friend%')
  AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM unnest(tag) AS t WHERE t LIKE '%cat%');

SELECT * FROM account_info 
WHERE condition::jsonb @> '[{"conditions": {"rootcompanyid": {"$in": [5]}}}]'
   OR condition::jsonb @> '[{"conditions": {"rootcompanyid": {"$in": [5, null]}}}]'
   OR condition::jsonb @> '[{"conditions": {"rootcompanyid": {"$in": [null, 5]}}}]'
   OR condition::jsonb @> '[{"conditions": {"rootcompanyid": {"$in": [null, 5, null]}}}]'
   OR condition::text LIKE '%"rootcompanyid": {"$in": [%5%]}%';

SELECT d.driverid, 
       COALESCE(SUM(r.points), 0) AS total_points
FROM drivers d
LEFT JOIN results r ON d.driverid = r.driverid
GROUP BY d.driverid
ORDER BY total_points DESC

SELECT version 
FROM cars 
ORDER BY 
    -- Major version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 1) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Minor version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 2) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Patch version
    CAST(SPLIT_PART(SPLIT_PART(version, '.', 3), '-', 1) AS INTEGER) DESC,
    -- Build number after the first hyphen
    CASE 
        WHEN POSITION('-' IN version) > 0 
        THEN CAST(NULLIF(SPLIT_PART(SUBSTRING(version FROM POSITION('-' IN version) + 1), '-', 1), '') AS INTEGER) 
        ELSE 0 
    END DESC,
    -- Any remaining text (like 'test' or 'dev') - sort alphabetically
    SUBSTRING(version FROM POSITION('-' IN version)) ASC
LIMIT 1;

WITH alignment_counts AS (
    SELECT A.alignment, COUNT(S.id) as total_count
    FROM superhero S
    JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
    GROUP BY A.alignment
)
SELECT 
    A.alignment,
    AC.total_count as total_by_alignment,
    R.race,
    COUNT(S.id) as count_by_alignment_race
FROM superhero S
JOIN alignment A ON S.alignment_id = A.id
JOIN race R ON S.race_id = R.id
JOIN alignment_counts AC ON A.alignment = AC.alignment
GROUP BY A.alignment, AC.total_count, R.race
ORDER BY A.alignment, R.race;

SELECT 
    label,
    JSON_AGG(
        JSON_BUILD_OBJECT(
            'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
            'atoms', (
                SELECT JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('atom_id', a.atom_id, 'element', a.element))
                FROM atom a
                WHERE a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
            ),
            'bonds', (
                SELECT JSON_AGG(JSON_BUILD_OBJECT('bond_id', b.bond_id, 'bond_type', b.bond_type))
                FROM bond b
                WHERE b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
            )
        )
    ) AS grouped_molecules
FROM 
    molecule m
GROUP BY 
    label

SELECT 
  raceid,
  COUNT(*) AS total_pitstops,
  SUM(CAST(SUBSTRING(duration FROM '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+') AS DECIMAL)) AS total_duration_seconds,
  -- Alternative approach using milliseconds column
  SUM(milliseconds)/1000.0 AS total_duration_seconds_from_ms
FROM pitstops
WHERE raceid = 842
GROUP BY raceid;

SELECT 
    driverId, 
    points, 
    lead(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "lead(driverId) with order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY points ASC) AS "array_agg(driverId) with order", 
    lead(driverId) OVER (ORDER BY driverId) AS "lead(driverId) without order", 
    array_agg(driverId) OVER () AS "array_agg(driverId) without order" 
FROM 
    results 
WHERE 
    raceId = 19 
ORDER BY 
    driverId ASC

SELECT 
    molecule_id, 
    COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'na' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS na_atoms,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'c' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
         AND SUM(CASE WHEN element = 'cl' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 
        THEN 1
        ELSE COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN element IN ('c', 'cl') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)
    END AS c_cl_atoms
FROM atom
GROUP BY molecule_id;

SELECT 
    home_team_api_id, 
    CAST(SUBSTR(date, 12, 2) AS INTEGER) AS hour_of_day,
    AVG(home_team_goal) as avg_home_goals, 
    AVG(away_team_goal) as avg_away_goals, 
    SUM(home_team_goal) as total_home_goals, 
    SUM(away_team_goal) as total_away_goals, 
    MAX(home_team_goal) as max_home_goals, 
    MIN(home_team_goal) as min_home_goals, 
    COUNT(home_team_api_id) as count 
FROM match 
GROUP BY home_team_api_id, CAST(SUBSTR(date, 12, 2) AS INTEGER)
ORDER BY home_team_api_id, hour_of_day;

SELECT array_to_json(array_agg(parent_with_children))
FROM (
    SELECT 
        p.id_module,
        p.desc_module,
        p.module_code,
        p.name_module,
        p.id_parent_module,
        (
            SELECT json_agg(children)
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    c.id_module,
                    c.desc_module,
                    c.module_code,
                    c.name_module,
                    c.id_parent_module
                FROM modules c
                WHERE c.id_parent_module = p.id_module
                ORDER BY c.id_module
            ) children
        ) AS children
    FROM modules p
    WHERE p.id_parent_module IS NULL
    ORDER BY p.id_module
) parent_with_children

WITH numbered_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    transaction_date,
    club_name,
    amount,
    sign(amount) AS amount_sign,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) - 
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY club_name, sign(amount) ORDER BY transaction_date) AS grp
  FROM club_transactions
),
grouped_transactions AS (
  SELECT 
    MIN(transaction_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(transaction_date) AS end_date,
    club_name,
    amount_sign,
    SUM(amount) AS total_amount
  FROM numbered_transactions
  GROUP BY club_name, amount_sign, grp
  ORDER BY club_name, start_date
)
SELECT 
  start_date,
  CASE WHEN start_date <> end_date THEN end_date ELSE NULL END AS end_date,
  club_name,
  total_amount
FROM grouped_transactions
ORDER BY club_name, start_date

INSERT INTO customers (customerid, segment, currency) 
VALUES (3, 'SME', 'EUR'), (1, 'KAM', 'CZK')
ON CONFLICT (customerid) DO UPDATE 
SET segment = EXCLUDED.segment, 
    currency = EXCLUDED.currency 
RETURNING customerid;

WITH unique_customers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT customerid, segment, currency
    FROM (VALUES 
        (3, 'SME', 'EUR'), 
        (1, 'KAM', 'CZK'), 
        (3, 'SME', 'EUR')
    ) AS t(customerid, segment, currency)
)
INSERT INTO customers (customerid, segment, currency)
SELECT customerid, segment, currency FROM unique_customers
ON CONFLICT (customerid) DO UPDATE 
SET segment = EXCLUDED.segment,
    currency = EXCLUDED.currency
RETURNING customerid;

SELECT 
    client.client_id, 
    client.gender, 
    disp.disp_id, 
    CASE 
        WHEN jsonb_typeof(disp.addresses) = 'array' THEN 
            jsonb_array_elements(disp.addresses) ->> 'PostCode'
        WHEN jsonb_typeof(disp.addresses) = 'object' THEN 
            disp.addresses ->> 'PostCode'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS PostCode
FROM 
    client 
JOIN 
    disp ON client.client_id = disp.client_id
WHERE 
    disp.client_id IN (100, 414)
    AND disp.addresses IS NOT NULL;

DELETE FROM cards 
WHERE id IN (
    SELECT c.id
    FROM cards c
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM norm1 n1
        WHERE n1.uuid = c.uuid 
        AND n1.setcode = c.setcode 
        AND n1.rarity = c.rarity 
        AND n1.manacost = c.manacost
        AND n1.uuid IS NOT NULL 
        AND n1.setcode IS NOT NULL 
        AND n1.rarity IS NOT NULL 
        AND n1.manacost IS NOT NULL
    )
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM norm2 n2
        WHERE n2.uuid = c.uuid 
        AND n2.setcode = c.setcode 
        AND n2.rarity = c.rarity 
        AND n2.manacost = c.manacost
        AND n2.uuid IS NOT NULL 
        AND n2.setcode IS NOT NULL 
        AND n2.rarity IS NOT NULL 
        AND n2.manacost IS NOT NULL
    )
    LIMIT 1000
)

)

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date)
RETURNS SETOF date 
LANGUAGE SQL 
IMMUTABLE 
PARALLEL SAFE 
ROWS 12 AS 
$function$
  SELECT generate_series(date_trunc('month', a), date_trunc('month', b), '1 month')
$function$;

-- Test the row estimate with direct function call
EXPLAIN SELECT generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');

-- Test the row estimate when used in FROM clause
EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM generate_series_monthly('2024-01-01', '2024-05-01');

WITH matches_with_draw_markers AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        home_team_goal + away_team_goal AS total_goals,
        CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_draw,
        SUM(CASE WHEN home_team_goal = away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY id, date) AS draw_group
    FROM match
),
goals_between_draws AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        is_draw,
        draw_group,
        SUM(total_goals) OVER (
            PARTITION BY draw_group
            ORDER BY id, date
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS running_total_goals
    FROM matches_with_draw_markers
)
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    home_team_goal,
    away_team_goal,
    running_total_goals AS amount
FROM goals_between_draws
WHERE is_draw = 1
ORDER BY id, date;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id_col text, order_col text) 
RETURNS void LANGUAGE plpgsql AS 
$$
DECLARE 
    col text;
    nullable_cols text[];
    update_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get all nullable columns from the table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name::text)
    INTO nullable_cols
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
    AND is_nullable = 'YES';
    
    -- For each nullable column, create and execute an update statement with last_value window function
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY nullable_cols
    LOOP
        update_stmt := format('
            UPDATE %I t1
            SET %I = t2.last_non_null_value
            FROM (
                SELECT 
                    %I as row_id,
                    %I,
                    LAST_VALUE(%I IGNORE NULLS) OVER (
                        PARTITION BY %I 
                        ORDER BY %I 
                        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                    ) as last_non_null_value
                FROM %I
            ) t2
            WHERE t1.%I = t2.row_id
            AND t1.%I IS NULL
            AND t2.last_non_null_value IS NOT NULL',
            tbl, col, id_col, id_col, col, id_col, order_col, tbl, id_col, col);
            
        EXECUTE update_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END;
$$;

WITH login_sessions AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action_time AS login_time,
        LEAD(action_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY action_time) AS next_login_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action = 'LOGIN'
),
search_actions AS (
    SELECT 
        s.user_id,
        s.action_time AS search_time,
        l.login_time,
        l.next_login_time
    FROM user_actions s
    JOIN login_sessions l ON s.user_id = l.user_id
        AND s.action_time > l.login_time
        AND (l.next_login_time IS NULL OR s.action_time < l.next_login_time)
    WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
),
buy_actions AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id,
        action_time AS buy_time
    FROM user_actions
    WHERE action = 'BUY'
)
SELECT 
    s.user_id,
    COUNT(*) AS abandoned_searches
FROM search_actions s
LEFT JOIN buy_actions b ON s.user_id = b.user_id
    AND b.buy_time > s.search_time
    AND (s.next_login_time IS NULL OR b.buy_time < s.next_login_time)
WHERE b.user_id IS NULL
GROUP BY s.user_id;

SELECT 
    ord.order_date, 
    ord.order_id, 
    ord.card_id, 
    prc.price
FROM 
    order_cards ord
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT price 
    FROM card_prices cp
    WHERE cp.card_id = ord.card_id 
      AND cp.start_date <= ord.order_date
    ORDER BY cp.start_date DESC
    LIMIT 1
) prc ON true

WITH deck_cards AS (
  SELECT unnest(card_order) AS card_id, 
         generate_subscripts(card_order, 1) AS position
  FROM decks 
  WHERE id = 1
)
SELECT c.id, c.name
FROM cards c
JOIN deck_cards dc ON c.id = dc.card_id
ORDER BY dc.position;

WITH LastTransPerStation AS (
    -- Get the last transaction for each customer-gasstation combination
    SELECT *
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            t.*,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC) as rn
        FROM transactions_1k t
    ) ranked
    WHERE rn = 1
),
CustomerIssueStations AS (
    -- For each customer, find the first gas station with amount < 10 (chronologically)
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        FIRST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid 
            ORDER BY date, time
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS first_issue_station
    FROM LastTransPerStation
    WHERE amount < 10
),
LastNormalStation AS (
    -- For each customer, find the last gas station with amount >= 10
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        LAST_VALUE(gasstationid) OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid 
            ORDER BY date, time
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
        ) AS last_normal_station
    FROM LastTransPerStation
    WHERE amount >= 10
)
SELECT 
    t.customerid,
    t.transactionid,
    t.gasstationid,
    t.amount
FROM LastTransPerStation t
LEFT JOIN CustomerIssueStations i ON t.customerid = i.customerid
LEFT JOIN LastNormalStation n ON t.customerid = n.customerid
WHERE 
    -- Include transaction if it's from the first station with an issue
    (i.first_issue_station = t.gasstationid)
    OR 
    -- Or if there's no issue station and it's the last normal station
    (i.first_issue_station IS NULL AND t.gasstationid = n.last_normal_station);

SELECT 
    player_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'goals' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS goals,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'assists' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS assists,
    MAX(CASE WHEN pos = 1 AND key = 'yellow_cards' THEN value ELSE NULL END) AS yellow_cards
FROM (
    SELECT 
        player_id,
        TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_keys, ','))) AS key,
        TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_values, ','))) AS value,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_id, TRIM(UNNEST(STRING_TO_ARRAY(stats_keys, ','))) ORDER BY player_id) AS pos
    FROM player_stats
) AS expanded
GROUP BY player_id
ORDER BY player_id;

UPDATE teams_config
SET configurations = jsonb_set(
    configurations,
    ARRAY[
        (SELECT (position - 1)::text
         FROM teams_config,
              jsonb_array_elements(configurations) WITH ORDINALITY arr(elem, position)
         WHERE elem->>'id' = '101'
         LIMIT 1)::text,
        'settings'
    ],
    '"updatedSettingValue"'::jsonb,
    true
)
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(configurations) AS elem
    WHERE elem->>'id' = '101'
);

SELECT 
    begin_date, 
    end_date,
    CASE 
        -- If begin_date is the first day of a month and end_date is the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1 AND 
              EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')))
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(end_date, begin_date)) * 12 + EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(end_date, begin_date))
        
        -- If begin_date is the first day of a month but end_date is not the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM begin_date) = 1)
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), begin_date)) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), begin_date))
        
        -- If begin_date is not the first day of a month but end_date is the last day of a month
        WHEN (EXTRACT(DAY FROM end_date) = EXTRACT(DAY FROM (DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date) + INTERVAL '1 month - 1 day')))
        THEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(end_date, DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(end_date, DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month'))
        
        -- If neither begin_date is the first day nor end_date is the last day of their respective months
        ELSE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month')) * 12 + 
             EXTRACT(MONTH FROM age(DATE_TRUNC('month', end_date), DATE_TRUNC('month', begin_date) + INTERVAL '1 month'))
    END AS full_calendar_months
FROM race_dates;

SELECT
    link_to_member,
    ROUND(SUM(hours) / 10) * 10 AS rounded_hours,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY ROUND(SUM(hours) / 10) * 10 DESC) AS rank
FROM attendance
GROUP BY link_to_member
ORDER BY rank, link_to_member;

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW findcount AS
SELECT
    season,
    CAST(findteam(season) AS INTEGER) AS team_count
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT season
    FROM match
    WHERE season >= '2008/2009' 
) seasons;

SELECT 
    posttypeid,
    SUM(CASE WHEN tags LIKE '%bayesian%' OR tags LIKE '%distributions%' 
        THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS keyword_count
FROM posts
GROUP BY posttypeid
ORDER BY posttypeid
